metadata.note: All budgets are HARD LIMITS. Exceeding by >10% = Gate 4 fail. Quality target: 0.03+ quality points per token.

prd.rationale: PRD: problem statement, user types, MVP features as user stories (max 5), acceptance criteria per feature, out-of-scope list, success metrics. No implementation detail — that's Architecture's job.

ux-research.rationale: UX Research: user flows (5 steps min each), screen names, key decisions, pain points. Diagram as text tree. No wireframes — describe component names only.

ui-spec.rationale: UI Spec: component list with names, layout description per screen, interaction states, design tokens (colors, spacing). Reference framework component library — don't invent names.

product-vision.rationale: Vision doc: one-liner, problem narrative (3 sentences max), 3-year target state, what success looks like. Short by design — this is for alignment, not detail.

data-schema.rationale: Data Schema: entity list, fields per entity (name + type + required + default), key relationships, indexes. Table format required. No prose explanations of field purpose.

dev-plan.rationale: Dev Plan: Phase 0 (setup) through MVP launch. Each task: file path + acceptance criterion + dependency. Phase milestones with testable definitions of done. No implementation guidance — just the task list.

starter-prompt.rationale: Starter Prompt: single self-contained prompt pasted into AI tool. What is being built (1 sentence), files provided (list), read CLAUDE.md first, Phase 0 Task 1. No explanatory prose.

claude-md-product.rationale: CLAUDE.md for generated product (not this docgen system). Follows 200-line rule. Sections: What This Is, Commands, Architecture, Code Style, Important Notes.

bundle-product.rationale: Total cap for the full 11-doc product bundle (PRD+UX+UI+Vision+ARCH+Schema+API+Setup+DevPlan+CLAUDE.md+Starter). Prevents scope creep across the full generation session.
bundle-product.note: If any single doc approaches its cap AND the bundle is approaching 22K, prioritize Dev Plan and Architecture — cut Vision and UX first.

project-setup.rationale: Full Claude project from idea: CLAUDE.md + project instructions + folder map + domain list. Complex output, higher budget justified.

claude-md.rationale: CLAUDE.md is a navigation index, not a content document. Must stay lean — Claude follows 200-line limit before compliance degrades.

project-instructions.rationale: Claude project instructions: role + context + format + 5 Dos + 5 Don'ts + examples. 500-800 words enforced by SYSTEM_Project_Instructions_Rules.md.

system-guide.rationale: System guides like SYSTEM_Exit_Rules.md: rules, examples, anti-patterns. Focused on one topic per file.

how-to.rationale: How-to guides: prerequisites, numbered steps with expected output, success table, 3-5 error signatures.

reference.rationale: Reference docs: complete coverage of a topic, tables, examples, all edge cases documented.

feature.rationale: Feature documentation: what it does, use cases, examples, limitations. Not a how-to.

process.rationale: Process docs: roles, steps, approvals, exceptions. One process per doc.

update.rationale: Update/improvement of existing doc. Should be smaller than the original — targeted changes only.

setup-guide.rationale: Code setup guides: prerequisites, installation steps, config, verification. Exact commands only.

api-guide.rationale: API docs: one endpoint per section, parameters table, request/response examples, error codes.

architecture.rationale: Architecture docs: system diagrams (text), component relationships, data flow, decisions rationale.

adr.rationale: ADR: context + decision + consequences + alternatives considered. Concise by design.

troubleshooting.rationale: Troubleshooting: 5-7 errors max, exact error signatures, root cause, fix steps. More go to DEBT.md.

config-reference.rationale: Config reference: all options, types, defaults, examples, validation rules.

schema-reference.rationale: Schema docs: field definitions, types, required/optional, examples, validation.

command-reference.rationale: Command reference: syntax, options, examples, error codes. Machine-parseable.

quality_target.description: Every doc must achieve 85+ quality score at or under its token budget
